title: check-status reports no version gap — which schemas are behind the catalog, and by how much, is not surfaced
description: checkStatus computes baseline_version and catalog_version per asset (CheckStatusAsset), but renderCheckStatus prints only the per-state name buckets and drops the version fields, and check-status is reachable only as an in-pi command (no CLI op — FGAP-030). No surface reports which schemas are behind the catalog or the version delta. A behind-by-version readout must also cover content-only drift: catalog-ahead is a content-hash comparison, so a schema can be behind with no version-number change.

evidence.reference: CheckStatusAsset carries baseline_version + catalog_version per asset
renderCheckStatus groups schema names by state and omits the version fields
checkStatus classifies catalog-ahead by content-hash comparison (catalogHash !== baseline), not version arithmetic

impact: From check-status the operator cannot see which schemas are behind the catalog or by how much — only the raw drift state, and only from inside pi.
proposed_resolution: check-status reports, per behind schema, the baseline->catalog version delta, and handles content-only drift (behind with no version-number change). Depends on FGAP-030 (expose check-status as a CLI op).
